title: Pharmacokinetics in Neonatal Medicine
authors: Hornik CD, Wade KC / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Pharmacokinetics?
(1) Neonates ≠ small adults — PK ไม่สามารถ extrapolate จาก adult หรือ older children ได้เลย; weight varies ≥10-fold (0.5–5 kg), GA varies 22–42 wks, postnatal age 1–150 days — variation ทุกมิติ; (2) Vd, CL, T½ ล้วน age-dependent — preterm ≠ term ≠ 3-week postnatal; dose ที่ถูกต้องสำหรับ 28-weeker วันแรกเกิด ผิดทันทีที่ postnatal age เปลี่ยน; (3) Therapeutic hypothermia ลด CL ของ phenobarbital, gentamicin, morphine — ถ้าไม่ปรับ dose → toxicity; (4) Most drugs used in NICU lack label dosing for neonates — off-label = clinician must understand PK principles (van den Anker J Clin Pharmacol 2018); (5) TDM (Therapeutic Drug Monitoring) ช่วยได้เมื่อเข้าใจ Kel, Vd, T½ — ถ้าไม่เข้าใจ TDM ก็แค่ตัวเลขที่ไม่มีความหมาย. PK knowledge = rational dose adjustment for every infant across the GA spectrum.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย PK/PD framework: dose → concentration → response; เป้าหมายของ antimicrobial therapy 3 ข้อ
อธิบาย ADME (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion) ในบริบท neonatal physiology
คำนวณ/อธิบาย Volume of Distribution (Vd) และเชื่อมกับ body composition ของ preterm vs term infant
อธิบาย Phase 1 (CYP450) และ Phase 2 metabolism; ontogeny ของ CYP3A4, CYP3A7, glucuronidation, sulfation
อธิบาย renal elimination ใน neonates: GFR development, tubular maturation, cystatin-C vs serum creatinine
แยกแยะ First-order kinetics vs Zero-order kinetics; One-compartment vs Two-compartment models
อธิบายผลของ Therapeutic Hypothermia ต่อ PK ของ ampicillin, gentamicin, phenobarbital, morphine, midazolam
ใช้ PK formulas (Kel, T½, Vd, CL) ในการคำนวณ dose adjustment — กรณีตัวอย่าง gentamicin
อธิบาย PK/PD targets สำหรับ antimicrobials: Cmax/MIC, AUC/MIC, %T>MIC — ยาตัวอย่างในแต่ละกลุ่ม
ระบุ indications สำหรับ TDM และ principles ของ bedside dose adjustment
1. PK/PD Framework — ทำไมต้องรู้ทั้งสองอย่าง
1.1 PK vs PD
Pharmacokinetics (PK): อธิบาย dose → concentration relationship; 'what the body does to the drug' — ADME
Pharmacodynamics (PD): อธิบาย concentration → effect relationship; 'what the drug does to the body' — efficacy + toxicity
Framework: Dose → [PK: CL, Vd, T½, Kel, Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion] → Concentration → [PD: receptor, MIC, effect target] → Response
Goal: design dose ที่ให้ drug exposure ที่ produce therapeutic response + minimize toxicity สำหรับ infant คนนั้นๆ โดยเฉพาะ
1.2 เป้าหมายของ Antimicrobial Therapy (3 ข้อ)
1. Eradicate the pathogen — kill or suppress organism
2. Minimize toxicity — ไม่ให้ concentration สูงเกินจนเป็น nephrotoxic, ototoxic, hepatotoxic
3. Prevent emergence of resistant organisms — ต้อง achieve adequate PK/PD target (Cmax/MIC, AUC/MIC, %T>MIC)
2. Drug Absorption — Route and Bioavailability
2.1 Bioavailability และ Route of Administration
Bioavailability (F): fraction ของ administered dose ที่ reach systemic circulation = AUC(non-IV) / AUC(IV); IV route → F = 100% by definition
Enteral bioavailability: ขึ้นกับ gastric acidity, gastric emptying time, intestinal absorption/transit, first-pass metabolism

First-pass metabolism: drug เข้า portal circulation → metabolized ใน liver ก่อน reach systemic circulation → ↓ bioavailability; preterm มี ↓ first-pass → ↑ bioavailability — เช่น midazolam enteral: F = 92% ใน preterm vs 30% ใน adults (Brussee CPT Pharmacometrics 2018)
IM absorption ใน neonates: ↓ muscle perfusion + contractility → ↓ absorption; unpredictable → prefer IV route เมื่อ possible

Percutaneous absorption: preterm → thin stratum corneum + ↑ surface area:body weight ratio + ↑ skin perfusion → large systemic exposure จาก topical medications; toxic levels reported จาก chlorhexidine, lidocaine, steroids
2.2 Neonatal-Specific Absorption Factors
Gastric acidity: neonates → ↓ gastric acid → ↑ absorption ของ acid-labile drugs (penicillin) แต่ ↓ absorption ของ weak organic acids (phenytoin, phenobarbital)
Gastric emptying time: ช้ากว่าใน preterm → ↑ time to Cmax; continuous enteral feeding → ↑ gastric pH → affects absorption
GI pathology: NEC, intestinal ischemia, ascites → ↓ enteral absorption อย่างมีนัยสำคัญ
Dose adjustment จาก IV → PO ต้องคำนึงถึง bioavailability difference อย่างละเอียด — ไม่ใช่ 1:1 เสมอ
3. Volume of Distribution (Vd) — แนวคิดหลักใน Neonatal PK
3.1 Definition และ Clinical Meaning
Vd (L/kg): hypothetical fluid volume ที่ drug กระจายอยู่ = Amount of drug given (mg/kg) ÷ [Plasma drug concentration] (mg/L)
Vd ไม่ใช่ anatomic volume — เป็น proportionality constant ที่ link dose กับ measured plasma concentration
Clinical implication: infant ที่มี Vd สูง → ต้องการ higher dose per kg เพื่อ achieve same peak concentration;
↑ Vd → ↓ Cmax → ↑ T½ (เพราะ T½ = 0.693 × Vd / CL)
3.2 Body Composition และ Vd ใน Neonates

Body Water and Vd: Preterm vs Term vs Adults
Total body water (TBW): fetus ~80% → preterm neonate ~75–80% → term neonate ~70% → adult ~60% body weight. Extracellular fluid (ECF): preterm ~55% → term ~45% → adult ~20% BW. Implication: Hydrophilic drugs (gentamicin, ampicillin, aminoglycosides) distribute in ECF → Vd much larger in preterm neonates → require higher mg/kg dose vs term infant to achieve same Cmax. Gentamicin: Vd ~0.5–0.6 L/kg in preterm vs ~0.4 L/kg in term. Lipophilic drugs: distribute in fat; very preterm (<26 wk, <5% body fat) → smaller Vd for lipophilic drugs (benzodiazepines, fentanyl) → ↑ free plasma concentration; LGA/diabetic mothers → ↑ body fat → ↑ Vd for lipophilic drugs.

Protein binding: neonates → ↓ albumin, ↓ alpha-1 acid glycoprotein → ↑ 'free drug' fraction for same total concentration → ↑ Vd + potential toxicity at 'normal' total drug levels; ↑ bilirubin + free fatty acids → displace drugs from albumin → ↑ free drug
Digoxin example: binds to skeletal + cardiac muscle proteins → very large Vd; infants + children have ↑ myocardial uptake → Vd larger in infants than adults — dosing per kg is higher
Excess fluid states: ascites, hydrops, cardiac edema → ↑↑ Vd for hydrophilic drugs → need higher loading doses; track fluid balance when interpreting drug levels
4. Drug Metabolism — Ontogeny ของ Enzymatic Pathways
4.1 Phase 1 Metabolism — CYP450 Enzymes
Phase 1: nonsynthetic — oxidation, reduction, hydrolysis, demethylation → polar metabolite; primarily CYP450 enzymes ใน liver
CYP3A4/3A5: metabolize ~50% of all medications; expressed ONLY AFTER BIRTH; activity ↑ rapidly in first months; preterm → ↓ CYP3A4 activity → ↓ CL of fentanyl, midazolam, tacrolimus
CYP3A7: fetal isoform — expressed ใน fetus แต่ ↓ after birth; substrate overlap กับ CYP3A4 → explains some fetal drug metabolism ที่ไม่ depend on CYP3A4

Fentanyl CYP3A4 example (Saarenmaa Pediatrics 2000): T½ = 317–1266 min ใน neonates (massive range); CL increases 3-fold in first 2 weeks; in first 48h: CL 40% higher at 41 wk GA vs 29 wk GA — ทุกวันสำคัญ

Drug-drug interactions via CYP: fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9 + CYP3A4 → ↑ levels phenobarbital/midazolam; phenobarbital induces CYP2B + CYP3A → ↑ CL of midazolam (ต้องเพิ่ม midazolam dose เมื่อใช้ร่วม phenobarbital)

CYP2D6 genetic polymorphism: codeine → morphine via CYP2D6; ultra-rapid metabolizer mothers → morphine toxicity in breastfed infant (case reports) — codeine contraindicated in lactation (Tobias Pediatrics 2016)
4.2 Phase 2 Metabolism — Conjugation Pathways
Phase 2: synthetic conjugation — glucuronidation (UGT), sulfation, acetylation, glutathione conjugation → highly polar, inactive, renally excreted metabolites

rows: ↓ morphine CL → accumulation; gray baby syndrome (chloramphenicol)
Sulfate conjugates dominant in neonates vs glucuronides in adults
Slow acetylators → ↑ caffeine half-life → less frequent dosing needed
Prolonged sedation; dose escalation needed as infant matures

blocks.text: Morphine glucuronidation (Knibbe Clin Pharmacokinet 2009): CL low ใน neonates; different glucuronide metabolites predominate (↑ M3G, ↓ M6G vs adults); CL ↑ with postnatal + postmenstrual age → dose escalation required
Acetaminophen (Flint Pediatr Res 2017): sulfation compensates for glucuronidation deficiency → sulfate conjugates = predominant form in neonates; generally safe in standard doses
Hepatic uptake, blood flow, body temperature, disease state ล้วน modify metabolism — not just enzyme maturity alone
5. Drug Excretion — Renal Maturation
5.1 Mechanisms of Renal Elimination
3 mechanisms: glomerular filtration + tubular secretion (proximal) + tubular reabsorption (distal)
Glomerular filtration (GFR): เป็น major pathway สำหรับ renally-eliminated drugs; dependent on renal blood flow + plasma protein binding (protein-bound drug ≠ filter freely)

GFR in preterm at 3 days (van den Anker J Clin Pharmacol 1995): increases significantly with advancing GA from 24 → 37 weeks; GFR doubles in first week of postnatal life → dose interval must be adjusted weekly ในช่วง แรก
Nephrogenesis: not complete until ~36 weeks PMA; preterm infants have ↓ nephrogenic potential → ↓ CL; major GFR increase occurs at ~34 weeks PMA (Rodieux Clin Pharmacokinet 2015)
5.2 Tubular Function
Tubular secretion: less developed in neonates — explains prolonged T½ of penicillin + furosemide; organic anion transport system matures postnatally
Tubular reabsorption: also delayed; Scr reabsorption across immature tubules → transient ↑ Scr in first days after birth → should not be misinterpreted as AKI
5.3 Biomarkers ของ Renal Function

Serum Creatinine ใน Neonates — Limitations
Scr ใน preterm: reflects maternal Scr ใน first few days; then tubular reabsorption → ↑ Scr transiently; subsequently ↓ with maturation. Problem: Scr requires time to accumulate — subtle ↓ GFR can ↓ drug CL BEFORE Scr rises. Alternative biomarkers: Cystatin-C: filtered by glomeruli, metabolized by proximal tubule cells, independent of muscle mass → rises faster than Scr when GFR ↓; more reliably predicted vancomycin concentrations in term infants (Shin Korean J Pediatr 2015). NGAL: urine biomarker for early AKI. KIM-1: transmembrane glycoprotein → urine in AKI only; ↑ levels associated with ↑ mortality in VLBW (Yim Pediatr Nephrol 2014). Clinical application: use these biomarkers + urine output trends to anticipate dose adjustment before Scr rises.
5.4 Disease States ที่ลด Renal CL
HIE, sepsis, AKI, congenital heart disease → ↓ GFR → delayed drug CL → need extended dosing intervals
Hepatic insufficiency: ↓ protein binding (↑ free drug) + ↓ hepatic blood flow + ↓ enzyme activity → ↑ CL variability; ↓ doses ที่ metabolized hepatically
Cardiac dysfunction: ↓ tissue perfusion + ↑ TBW → alter Vd + CL of all drugs unpredictably
6. PK Models — First-Order, Zero-Order, Compartments
6.1 First-Order vs Zero-Order Kinetics

rows: Constant PERCENTAGE eliminated per unit time
Constant AMOUNT eliminated per unit time
Exponential decay; linear on log scale
Linear decay on linear scale; non-linear on log scale
Depends on dose — larger dose → longer T½
Most NICU drugs: ampicillin, gentamicin, phenobarbital
Phenytoin, alcohol; any drug at saturating doses

blocks.text: Phenytoin zero-order: saturable CYP2C9 kinetics; small dose increase → disproportionate ↑ levels → toxicity; T½ is dose-dependent; switch to first-order when concentrations below saturating threshold
6.2 One-Compartment vs Two-Compartment Models

One-compartment: drug distributes instantaneously to single homogeneous volume; concentration ↓ linearly on log scale with one rate constant (Kel); simplified but useful for many NICU drugs with short distribution phase

Two-compartment: biphasic elimination — alpha phase (rapid distribution) + beta phase (slower elimination); semi-log plot shows two distinct slopes; required for drugs with significant peripheral tissue distribution (digoxin, amiodarone)
Practical implication: gentamicin approximates one-compartment model IF peak drawn 30 min after end of infusion (during beta phase) → simplified Kel, T½, Vd, CL calculations valid
7. Therapeutic Hypothermia — PK Alterations
7.1 Mechanisms of PK Change in Cooling
HIE + therapeutic hypothermia ↓ core temperature <34°C for 72h → compound PK changes:
Organ injury (HIE itself): ↓ cardiac output → ↓ renal + hepatic perfusion → ↓ drug elimination
Hypothermia direct effect: systemic CL of hepatic CYP450-metabolized drugs ↓ 7–22% per °C below 37°C (Tortorici Crit Care Med 2007); enzyme kinetics slow → ↑ T½ for almost all drugs
Hemodynamic changes: ↓ GFR in early postnatal hours → ↓ renal drug CL
7.2 Drug-Specific Changes During Hypothermia

rows: Bijleveld Br J Clin Pharmacol 2016; Frymoyer J Perinatol 2013
Extend interval to q36h; use TDM; space doses for washout
↑ T½ from 4–6 days → 8–22 days; severity of asphyxia = covariate
Filippi Epilepsia 2011; Shellhaas Pediatr Crit Care Med 2013
↑ exposure → worse NDO; TDM essential; reduce dose or extend interval
Favie PLoS One 2019; Frymoyer J Clin Pharmacol 2017
Loading dose + low maintenance infusion to prevent accumulation
Reduce dose if used without phenobarbital; ↑ dose if used with phenobarbital (CYP3A induction)
Adjust dose interval; monitor for accumulation

blocks.text:
Phenobarbital + Midazolam Interaction ใน Cooling
Phenobarbital เป็น CYP3A inducer → ↑ CL ของ midazolam → ถ้า phenobarbital + midazolam ร่วมกันใน cooling → midazolam CL มากกว่าที่คาด → ต้องใช้ dose midazolam สูงกว่าเพื่อ achieve therapeutic effect. แต่ถ้า midazolam ใช้โดยไม่มี phenobarbital → CL ต่ำ (CYP3A ยังไม่ถูก induced) → ลด midazolam dose เพื่อ prevent accumulation (van den Broek Neonatology 2015; Favie Neonatology 2019). Critical point: drug interaction + cooling = double jeopardy สำหรับ accumulation.
8. PK Formulas สำหรับ Bedside Calculations
8.1 Core Parameters และ Equations
Kel (h⁻¹): elimination rate constant = slope ของ log concentration-time curve = Ln[C1/C2] / Δt
T½ (h): 0.693 / Kel หรือ (0.693 × Vd) / CL — time ที่ใช้ให้ concentration ลดลงครึ่งหนึ่ง; after 4–5 T½ = steady state achieved; after 4–5 T½ = >97% drug eliminated
Vd (L/kg): Dose (mg/kg) / [Cmax] (mg/L); Cmax measured 30 min after end of infusion for gentamicin (ensures in beta phase)
CL (L/h·kg): Kel × Vd หรือ (0.693 × Vd) / T½; at steady state: CL = Dose / (τ × [drug]ss)
8.2 Gentamicin — Worked Example (Box 47.1 Summary)

Case A: Mary — 40 wk Term, 4 mg/kg q24h
Peak (30 min post-infusion) = 10 mg/L; Trough (1h before next dose, at 22h after peak) = 0.3 mg/L. Step 1 — Kel: Kel = Ln[10/0.3] / 22h = Ln(33.3) / 22 = 3.506 / 22 = 0.159 h⁻¹. Step 2 — T½: T½ = 0.693 / 0.159 = 4.4h. Step 3 — Vd: Vd = 4 mg/kg / 10 mg/L = 0.4 L/kg. Step 4 — CL: CL = 0.159 × 0.4 = 0.064 L/(h·kg). Interpretation: Normal term PK. Peak 10 mg/L ≥10× typical MIC (1 mg/L) → adequate Cmax/MIC. Trough <1 mg/L → washout before next dose.

Case C: Ava — 25 wk Preterm, 500g — How PK Changes with Prematurity
Peak (4 mg/kg) = 6.8 mg/L; Trough at 22h = 3 mg/L → Kel = Ln[6.8/3] / 22 = 0.82 / 22 = 0.037 h⁻¹; T½ = 0.693 / 0.037 = 18.7h; Vd = 4/6.8 = 0.59 L/kg; CL = 0.037 × 0.59 = 0.022 L/(h·kg). Optimal dose for Ava: 5 mg/kg q48h → Peak ~8.5 mg/L; Trough ~1.3 mg/L at 46h. Comparison vs Mary: CL threefold reduced; Vd 1.5× higher; T½ fourfold longer → dose must be larger mg/kg AND interval much longer. After 3 doses: gentamicin ACCUMULATES in Ava → repeat TDM warranted if treatment continued.
Rule: ≥4–5 T½ needed to reach steady state; for Ava (T½ ~19h) → steady state at ~90h (~4 days); TDM early in course to guide adjustment
9. Pharmacodynamics — PK/PD Targets สำหรับ Antimicrobials
9.1 PK/PD Exposure Targets
MIC (Minimum Inhibitory Concentration): lowest drug concentration that inhibits visible growth ของ organism; higher MIC = harder to achieve target
3 exposure targets relative to MIC:
Cmax/MIC: bacterial killing proportional to maximal concentration vs MIC — concentration-dependent killing
%T>MIC: % of dosing interval that [drug] stays above MIC — time-dependent killing; no concentration dependence
AUC/MIC: total drug exposure vs MIC — time-dependent killing with post-antibiotic effect

rows: Optimize total exposure; TDM-guided AUC targeting

blocks.text: Ampicillin example: time-dependent — effective killing starts at 2×MIC; >4×MIC adds no benefit; no post-exposure activity; needs q8-12h or continuous infusion to maintain %T>MIC ≥50%

Vancomycin 2020 consensus (Rybak J Pediatric Infect Dis Soc 2020): AUC/MIC-guided TDM preferred over trough-only monitoring; AUC 400–600 mg·h/L for MRSA invasive infections (AAP/ASHP/IDSA consensus); TDM-guided approach ↓ nephrotoxicity vs trough-guided
10. Drug Reference — PK Properties ใน Neonates

rows: Cmax/MIC target; TDM peak+trough; q36h in cooling
T½ 8–22 days in cooling; TDM essential; ↓ NDO if high levels
↑ T½ in cooling; loading + low infusion; watch resp depression
Phenobarbital ↑ CL; cooling ↓ CL; complex interaction
Huge PK range; CL ↑ 3× first 2 wk; ↑ fat → ↑ Vd in LGA
AUC/MIC-guided TDM (2020 consensus); ↑ T½ if AKI
Wide T½ range PT; loading 20mg/kg; maintenance 5–10mg/kg/d

blocks.text: 11. NICU Practical — PK ที่ Bedside
11.1 Gentamicin TDM Protocol

Extended-interval dosing rationale: Cmax/MIC ≥10:1 → concentration-dependent killing + post-antibiotic effect; extended interval → washout → ↓ renal accumulation (saturable uptake); ↓ nephrotoxicity (Verpooten Clin Pharmacol Ther 1989)
Peak timing: draw 30 min after end of infusion → ensures in beta phase → valid Kel calculation
Trough timing: 1 hour before next dose → if trough high (>2 mg/L) → extend interval, do not reduce dose (you'll compromise Cmax/MIC)
Cooling patients: q36h as starting interval regardless of GA; TDM after 1st dose + adjust; levels may rise progressively → repeat after 2nd dose
11.2 Vancomycin TDM — AUC-Guided
2020 guideline shift: move from trough-only (goal 10–15 mg/L) → AUC/MIC targeting (AUC 400–600 mg·h/L ÷ MIC); trough-only approach overestimates nephrotoxicity risk
Practical: obtain peak (1h post-infusion) + trough → calculate AUC via PK software or Bayesian estimation; some NICUs use 2-level AUC calculation manually
Cystatin-C may predict vancomycin level changes before Scr rises → use trend ใน sick infants
11.3 Checklist ก่อน Prescribe ใน NICU
(1) GA + postnatal age → estimate Vd + CL for this drug at this developmental stage
(2) Fluid status → ascites/hydrops? → ↑ Vd? → adjust loading dose
(3) Renal/hepatic function → ↓ GFR? → extend interval; liver disease → ↓ hepatic CL drugs
(4) Drug interactions → CYP inducers/inhibitors?
(5) Therapeutic hypothermia? → extend intervals; consider loading + low-dose infusion for morphine/phenobarbital
(6) Route conversion IV→PO → account for bioavailability difference; not 1:1
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Neonates ≠ Small Adults: ต้องปรับ Dose ทุก Milestone
PK ใน neonates ไม่สามารถ extrapolate จาก adult data ได้เลย. Vd, CL, T½ เปลี่ยนตาม GA, postnatal age, body composition, organ function. Rules of thumb: (1) Preterm → larger Vd hydrophilic drugs → higher loading dose mg/kg; (2) Preterm + postnatal age <1 wk → ↓ CL → extend dosing interval; (3) ทุก 2 wk postnatal age → reassess interval; ทุก 4 wk reassess dose. Fentanyl CL ↑ 3× in first 2 weeks — infant ที่ sedated ดี day 1 อาจ under-sedated day 14 ด้วย dose เดิม.

PEARL 2 — Therapeutic Hypothermia ↓ CL ของยาเกือบทุกตัว
During cooling: phenobarbital T½ ↑ จาก 4–6 days → 8–22 days; gentamicin → extend q36h; morphine CL ↓ 20.7%; midazolam ↑ T½. Mechanism: (1) organ injury จาก HIE → ↓ renal + hepatic perfusion; (2) hypothermia itself → ↓ CYP enzyme kinetics ~7–22% per °C below 37°C. Action: start extended intervals proactively, use TDM early, watch for accumulation especially phenobarbital (↑ levels → worse NDO). After rewarming: CL improves → may need to ↑ doses.

PEARL 3 — Gentamicin Dosing: Bigger + Less Frequent = Better
Gentamicin = concentration-dependent killing; goal Cmax/MIC ≥10:1. Extended-interval dosing: ↑ Cmax → ↑ efficacy; washout trough (<2 mg/L, ideally <1 mg/L) → ↓ renal accumulation (saturable uptake) → ↓ nephrotoxicity (Verpooten 1989). Preterm 25 wk (Ava): 5 mg/kg q48h >> 4 mg/kg q24h. If trough is HIGH → extend interval, do NOT reduce dose (reducing dose kills Cmax/MIC). In cooling → q36h universal regardless of GA.

PEARL 4 — Serum Creatinine ไม่ใช่ Early Marker: ใช้ Trend + Cystatin-C
Scr ใน preterm สะท้อน maternal Scr ใน first days; transient tubular reabsorption → ↑ Scr เองในช่วงแรก. ↓ GFR อาจเกิดก่อน Scr ↑ โดยหลายชั่วโมง — in that window, drug CL ลดแล้วแต่ Scr ยัง 'normal'. Cystatin-C ↑ faster than Scr when GFR falls; more reliably predicts vancomycin concentration (Shin 2015). Practical: use urine output trend + cystatin-C trend + drug levels together; เริ่ม empiric dose adjustment เมื่อ urine output ↓ โดยไม่ต้องรอ Scr

PEARL 5 — Beta-Lactams: Time > Concentration — ต้องให้ถี่หรือ Infuse Continuously
Ampicillin, cefotaxime, meropenem = time-dependent killing: efficacy = %T>MIC ≥50–100% dosing interval. ↑ dose ≠ ↑ efficacy (อยู่แล้ว saturated); ↑ frequency หรือ continuous infusion = เพิ่ม %T>MIC. ทำไม q12h standard ใน neonates ≤7 days: T½ ยาวพอที่ q12h ยังให้ adequate %T>MIC. แต่เมื่อ postnatal age >7 days → T½ ลดลง → อาจต้อง q8h เพื่อ maintain coverage. สำหรับ meningitis หรือ resistant organism: continuous infusion เหมาะที่สุดสำหรับ beta-lactam efficacy.
13. Evidence Update — Key Studies
van den Anker J et al J Clin Pharmacol 2018;58(suppl 10):ES10: comprehensive review developmental pharmacokinetics: maturational changes in Vd, CL, T½ across preterm → term → infant
Allegaert K et al Curr Pharm Des 2017;23:5769: neonatal developmental PK: maturational changes and beyond — clinical framework for dose selection
Brussee JM et al CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol 2018;7:374: midazolam enteral: bioavailability 92% in preterm vs 30% adults — ↓ first-pass hepatic metabolism in preterm
Knibbe CA et al Clin Pharmacokinet 2009;48:371: morphine glucuronidation preterm neonates vs infants/children <3y: CL low, metabolite profile different, increases with postnatal + PMA
Flint RB et al Pediatr Res 2017;82:678: IV acetaminophen 10/15/20 mg/kg in very preterm: sulfate conjugates dominant vs adults; glucuronidation develops postnatal age
Tobias JD et al Pediatrics 2016;138(4): codeine in children/breastfeeding: CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers → morphine toxicity in infant; AAP/FDA recommend against codeine in lactating mothers
Saarenmaa E et al J Pediatr 2000;136:767: GA + birth weight effects on fentanyl plasma clearance: CL increases with GA; CL 40% higher at 41 vs 29 wk in first 48h
van den Anker JN et al Clin Pharmacol Ther 1995;58:650: ceftazidime PK in preterm: effects of renal function + GA on CL — GFR increases with GA; major surge at ~34 wk PMA
Shin JE et al Korean J Pediatr 2015;58:421: cystatin-C more reliably predicted serum vancomycin in term infants vs Scr — supports cystatin-C as functional GFR marker
Tortorici MA et al Crit Care Med 2007;35:2196: hypothermia ↓ CYP450-mediated CL 7–22% per °C below 37°C in adults — used as framework for neonatal cooling PK
Bijleveld YA et al Br J Clin Pharmacol 2016;81:1067: gentamicin PK in cooling: ↓ CL, ↑ T½; support for q36h dosing protocol
Frymoyer A et al J Perinatol 2013;33:778: gentamicin q36h dosing in HIE + cooling: effective and prevents accumulation
Filippi L et al Epilepsia 2011;52:794: phenobarbital PK during whole-body hypothermia: T½ 8–22 days vs 4.3 days in term without cooling; ↑ exposure → dose adjustment essential
Shellhaas RA et al Pediatr Crit Care Med 2013;14:194: population PK phenobarbital HIE in cooling: severity of asphyxia = important covariate of CL — TDM required
Favie LMA et al PLoS One 2019;14:e0211910: morphine PK in cooling: CL ↓ 20.7%; loading + low maintenance recommended
Favie LMA et al Neonatology 2019;116:154: phenobarbital + midazolam PK interaction in cooling: phenobarbital induces CYP3A → ↑ midazolam CL when used together
Verpooten GA et al Clin Pharmacol Ther 1989;45:22: once-daily dosing ↓ renal accumulation of gentamicin + netilmicin (saturable uptake) → supports extended-interval rationale
Rybak MJ et al J Pediatric Infect Dis Soc 2020;9:281: vancomycin TDM consensus: AUC/MIC-guided monitoring preferred over trough-only; AUC 400–600 for MRSA invasive infections
14. Self-Check Q&A
Q: ทำไม Gentamicin dose ของ preterm 25 wk (Ava) ต้องสูงกว่า mg/kg และ interval ยาวกว่า เมื่อเทียบกับ term (Mary)?

ตอบ: Vd ↑ ใน preterm: body water 75–80% vs 70% term → Vd 0.59 vs 0.40 L/kg → loading dose ต้องสูงกว่า mg/kg เพื่อ achieve adequate Cmax. CL ↓ ใน preterm: GFR immature → ↓ renal CL → T½ 19h vs 4.4h → ถ้าใช้ interval q24h → trough ≥3 mg/L → accumulation → nephrotoxicity + ototoxicity. Solution: 5 mg/kg q48h for 25-weeker → Peak ~8.5, Trough ~1.3 mg/L. TDM after dose 1 and 2 เพราะหลังขนาดที่ 3 drug เริ่ม accumulate.
Q: อธิบาย First-order kinetics vs Zero-order kinetics ด้วยตัวอย่างยา

ตอบ: First-order: constant % removed per unit time → exponential decay; T½ คงที่ไม่ขึ้นกับ dose; most NICU drugs (gentamicin, ampicillin, phenobarbital). Zero-order: constant AMOUNT removed per unit time; T½ ขึ้นกับ dose → ↑ dose → ↑ T½ disproportionately; phenytoin = classic example (saturable CYP2C9); alcohol. Clinical risk: small dose increase of phenytoin → large ↑ concentration → toxicity. Also: any first-order drug given at very high dose can saturate enzymes → transiently behave zero-order.
Q: Midazolam ใน cooling: ทำไมการ co-prescribe phenobarbital เปลี่ยน dose ที่ต้องการ?

ตอบ: Phenobarbital induces CYP3A → ↑ CL ของ midazolam → ระดับ midazolam ↓ → ต้องใช้ dose สูงกว่าเพื่อ achieve therapeutic effect. ถ้าไม่มี phenobarbital: midazolam CL ต่ำ (CYP3A ยัง under-induced) + cooling → ↑ T½ → accumulation risk → ↓ midazolam dose. Practical: always assess what other drugs are on board before adjusting midazolam. If stopping phenobarbital → midazolam CL drops immediately → reassess infusion rate.
Q: Beta-lactam antibiotics เช่น ampicillin ใช้หลัก PK/PD อะไร? ทำไมการเพิ่ม dose ไม่ช่วย?

ตอบ: %T>MIC ≥50%: bacterial killing proportional to TIME above MIC, not peak concentration. Efficacy starts at 2×MIC → increasing beyond 4×MIC adds no further bactericidal benefit; no post-antibiotic effect. ดังนั้น: ↑ dose ไม่ช่วย — ↑ frequency หรือ continuous infusion = strategy ที่ถูก. NEC หรือ meningitis: continuous infusion อาจดีที่สุด. Neonatal q12h (≤7 days) ถูกต้องเพราะ T½ ยาวพอ; หลัง 7 days → T½ ลด → อาจ q8h.
Q: Serum creatinine มีข้อจำกัดอย่างไรใน neonate? biomarker ทางเลือกคืออะไร?

ตอบ: (1) Scr สะท้อน maternal Scr ใน first days (crosses placenta); (2) tubular reabsorption ใน immature kidney → ↑ Scr transiently at birth; (3) Scr requires accumulation time → acute ↓ GFR may ↓ drug CL hours before Scr ↑. Alternatives: Cystatin-C: GFR-dependent only; ↑ faster than Scr (Shin 2015); not affected by muscle mass. NGAL (urine): early AKI. KIM-1 (urine): specific for tubular injury; ↑ mortality in VLBW. Best practice: combine urine output + cystatin-C trend + drug levels.
Q: ทำไม preterm infant ที่ใช้ midazolam มักมี levels สูงกว่าที่คาด เมื่อเทียบกับ term?

ตอบ: (1) CYP3A4 expressed only after birth + activity ↑ postnatal age → preterm มี ↓ CYP3A4 activity → ↓ hepatic CL; (2) ↓ first-pass metabolism → ↑ bioavailability ถ้าให้ enteral; (3) ↑ Vd (↑ body water) → ↑ T½; (4) cooling → compounds all above. Result: midazolam accumulates in preterm → sedation + ↑ risk hypotension, respiratory depression. Lesson: start low, titrate, use TDM (levels available in some centers).
Q: AUC/MIC-guided vancomycin TDM คืออะไร และต่างจาก trough-only อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Trough-only (old approach): trough goal 10–15 mg/L → overcorrects toward high troughs → ↑ nephrotoxicity without proportional ↑ efficacy. AUC/MIC (2020 consensus Rybak): AUC 400–600 mg·h/L ÷ MIC (MRSA MIC usually 1 mg/L) → AUC 400–600 mg·h/L total. Method: obtain peak (1h post-infusion) + trough → calculate AUC using 2-level PK method or Bayesian software → ↓ nephrotoxicity vs trough-only, non-inferior efficacy. Practical for neonates: apply same principle; adjust dose interval based on AUC estimate; higher Vd in preterm → often need larger dose to reach same AUC.
Q: Phenobarbital ใน HIE cooling: ทำไมต้อง TDM และ consequence ของ high levels คืออะไร?

ตอบ: PK change: T½ ↑ 8–22 days (vs 4.3 days term without cooling); CL ↓ due to CYP2C9 ↓ + renal impairment; severity of HIE = covariate of CL (Pokorn Pediatrics 2019). Consequence of high levels: ↑ phenobarbital exposure → worse neurodevelopmental outcomes (Shellhaas Pediatr Crit Care Med 2013) — not just sedation but neurotoxicity in developing brain. Action: TDM essential after every 2–3 doses; if levels rising → extend interval or reduce dose; target serum level 20–40 µg/mL; recheck after rewarming since CL ↑.

Day 145 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.48
บทต่อไป: Chapter 48 — Infants of Substance-Using Mothers. เตรียมตัว: Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome (NAS) / Neonatal Opioid Withdrawal Syndrome (NOWS) — opioid, alcohol, nicotine, stimulants; Finnegan NAS scoring; modified Finnegan; Eat Sleep Console (ESC) tool; pharmacotherapy: morphine vs methadone vs buprenorphine; adjuncts (phenobarbital, clonidine); prenatal drug exposure → fetal neurodevelopment effects; PDPA-sensitive approach สำหรับ family.
